message:
feat(btree): BwTree-style delta chains for hot-row UPDATE (IMPL-23 / AG-1B)

Introduces the standalone BwTree (Levandoski, Lomet & Sengupta 2013) delta-chain
primitive that stages per-column UPDATEs as immutable delta nodes instead of
rewriting the full row each time. Hot rows (same rowid updated many times) can
amortize their bookkeeping cost across N updates and pay the full row-rewrite
price only when the chain crosses a consolidation threshold.

Module: crates/fsqlite-btree/src/delta_chain.rs (330 lines)

Primitives:
* DeltaNode { column_idx: u16, new_value: SqliteValue, prev: Option<Arc<Self>> }
* HotRowDeltaChain { base: Arc<Vec<SqliteValue>>, head: Option<Arc<DeltaNode>>, depth: u32 }
* apply_update(chain, col, val)    — push a new delta onto the head
* materialize(chain)               — head-to-base walk, latest-writer wins per column
* should_consolidate(chain, thr)   — depth >= threshold
* consolidate(chain)               — fresh Arc<Vec<SqliteValue>> base

Design notes:
* head is Option so a freshly-constructed chain is deltaless (materialize == base).
* All links are Arc so readers can traverse without cloning per-delta state.
* Materialization tracks a per-column "written" bitmap so older deltas for a
  column that has a newer write are skipped on the walk.
* consolidate() is non-mutating: it returns a new base and leaves the chain
  untouched, so the eventual integration layer can decide whether to swap in
  place or via copy-on-write under its preferred locking discipline.

Tests (8, all passing):
1. empty chain materializes to base
2. single UPDATE visible in materialize
3. repeated UPDATEs to same column → latest wins
4. UPDATEs to different columns → all applied
5. consolidate preserves the logical row
6. should_consolidate threshold boundary (N-1 false, N true)
7. deep 500-node chain materializes correctly (no stack overflow)
8. proptest: 1..100 random (col, value) writes on a 10-column row, consolidate
   every 5 deltas, assert materialize matches a ground-truth naive-rewrite row
   both before and after each consolidation

Integration plan (NOT in this commit):
* Wire into UPDATE via a hot-row eligibility gate that tracks per-rowid update
  frequency and only switches to delta-chain mode above a hotness threshold.
* Coordinate with index maintenance: an indexed-column delta must still touch
  the affected index (or defer index rebuild until consolidation, which is a
  separate correctness argument).
* Consolidation policy: thread the threshold through the page-level cooling
  state machine so chain depth is bounded even for pathological workloads.
* Persistence: the current primitive lives in memory only; a durable form
  would need a redo-log entry per delta before the write is acknowledged.

No unsafe. No changes to connection.rs. No changes to the UPDATE path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
